title: OPL Structured Claim (v2)
description: Canonical structured-claim object emitted by the claim-assembly / claim_audit prompts and consumed by the mechanical reasoning-quality gates. v2 (ADR-0026 P1/P2, session 0d1017d4 KRAS-G12C/MSS-mCRC findings). CONTRACT: the LLM (via prompt) makes the clinical judgment and records it in the structured fields below; the gates are MECHANICAL — they verify the judgment was recorded and is internally coherent, never re-make it. A gate SKIPs (non-blocking) when the field it needs is absent, so a field being optional means 'gate cannot judge', not 'safe'. Field names here are load-bearing: prompt authors MUST emit these exact keys and gate authors MUST read these exact keys. `entities` and `evidence[].{type,id,quote}` already match what G36 consumes today — do not rename. See prompts/tasks/claim_audit.md for the producer contract and src/opl_cancer/validators/gates/g3[4-7,9]_*.py / g4[0-3]_*.py for consumers.

claim_id.description: REQUIRED. Stable id for this claim within the run (e.g. 'c_001'). Used by claim_audit cross-claim self-contradiction pass and by all gate evidence payloads to point back at the offending claim.

claim_text.description: REQUIRED. The verbatim claim sentence as it will appear (or did appear) in the brief. Gates quote excerpts from this.

level.description: OPL research level 0-4 (0=patient-context fact … 3=world-unknown hypothesis, 4=speculative/redacted). Used by G8 level3/4-disclosure. NOT the evidence tier — see `tier`.

tier.description: The claim's OWN overall three-tier evidence label. Distinct from `evidence[].tier` (per-link). G42 enforces the tier-floor invariant: a claim's `tier` cannot exceed the strongest tier present in its `evidence[]` (an 'established' claim must have ≥1 'established' evidence link; you cannot launder exploratory evidence into an established headline).

entities.description: Structured topical entities the claim is about: gene / variant / drug / cancer-type tokens (e.g. ['KRAS', 'G12C', 'sotorasib', 'colorectal']). ALREADY CONSUMED BY G36 (pmid_topic_relevance) to judge whether a cited PMID is on-topic. Do not rename. Must be the producer's extracted entities, never a hardcoded disease keyword list (memory:feedback_default_prompt_over_script).

evidence.description: Per-link evidence backing the claim. Each link carries its OWN tier so G42 can compute the tier floor. The {type,id,quote} shape ALREADY matches what G36 reads — do not rename those three keys.

type.description: Evidence source kind. 'pmid'/'nct' are the canonical anchors; G36 only relevance-checks type=='pmid'.

id.description: The identifier (e.g. '32366523' for a PMID, 'NCT04185883' for a trial). Bare id, no '[PMID:]' wrapper.

quote.description: Verbatim supporting quote from the source (used by G2 quote-match and claim_audit consistency check). Empty/absent ⇒ those checks skip this link.

tier.description: The evidence strength of THIS link (RCT/meta/FDA-label ⇒ established; single cohort/case ⇒ exploratory; mechanistic/preclinical ⇒ speculative). G42 takes the strongest link tier as the ceiling for the claim's `tier`.

drugs_mentioned.description: INN (generic) drug names the claim invokes (e.g. ['sotorasib', 'panitumumab']). ALREADY USED BY Henry. G40 (comorbidity-safety) looks up each name against the curated FDA-label contraindication reference and checks that any triggered contraindication-class was addressed in `comorbidity_safety`.

regimen.description: Present when the claim proposes a treatment regimen. G39 (biomarker-contingency) fires here: a headline / top-ranked regimen must NOT be gated on a biomarker whose patient_state is unknown/untested. The LLM records the biomarker dependency; G39 mechanically checks that a headline regimen's required biomarkers all have a known patient_state (and that required_state is satisfied).

is_headline.description: True if this is a headline / primary recommended regimen (vs. a secondary/contingent option). G39 only blocks on headline regimens — a clearly-labelled contingent option gated on an unknown biomarker is legitimate ('IF MSI-H is confirmed, consider …').

rank.description: 1-based recommendation rank. rank==1 is treated as headline by G39 even if is_headline is unset.

required_biomarkers.description: Biomarkers this regimen is contingent on. Each entry pairs the required state with the patient's KNOWN state. If patient_state is unknown/untested/null and the regimen is headline, G39 blocks (the KRAS-G12C/MSS finding: a headline regimen presented as if a yet-unmeasured biomarker were already favourable).

required_state.description: The biomarker state the regimen REQUIRES (e.g. 'MSI-H', 'G12C', 'positive', 'wild-type').

patient_state.description: The patient's ACTUAL known state for this biomarker, or null / 'unknown' / 'untested' if not measured. G39 blocks a headline regimen when this is null/unknown/untested, or when it is known and does NOT satisfy required_state.

comorbidity_safety.description: The LLM's record that it considered the patient's comorbidities against the claim's drugs. G40 (comorbidity-safety) checks this against the curated FDA-label drug→contraindication-class reference: if a drug in `drugs_mentioned` carries a contraindication class (e.g. QT-prolongation, hepatotoxicity, nephrotoxicity) the relevant comorbidity must appear in `comorbidities_considered` (or `addressed` must be explicitly true with a note). Absent ⇒ G40 SKIPs (cannot judge) — the producer prompt is what guarantees this is populated whenever drugs_mentioned is non-empty.

addressed.description: True if the producer affirms it weighed the patient's comorbidities against this claim's drugs.

comorbidities_considered.description: The specific patient comorbidities the producer evaluated (e.g. ['long QT', 'cirrhosis', 'CKD stage 3']). G40 checks the contraindication-class triggered by a drug maps to one of these.

note.description: Free-text rationale (non-directive). Surfaced in the G40 evidence payload.

soc_checklist.description: Standard-of-care checklist for the claim's clinical setting: each guideline-warranted item with whether the claim/brief addressed it. G41 (soc-completeness) checks that no item is left status=='missing' without a note explaining why (the under-delivery failure mode — a SoC option silently dropped). 'na' (not applicable to this patient) with a note is fine.

item.description: The SoC element (e.g. 'germline testing', 'consider anti-EGFR if RAS wild-type', 'palliative care referral').

status.description: addressed = covered in the brief; missing = warranted but not covered; na = not applicable to this patient. G41 blocks/warns on 'missing' without a note.

note.description: Why missing/na, or where addressed. Required (non-empty) when status is 'missing' or 'na'.

skepticism.description: The producer's record of how it applied skepticism across evidence — used by G43 (skepticism-symmetry) to catch motivated reasoning: dismissing inconvenient evidence on grounds it tolerates for relied-upon evidence. The LLM makes the call (which refs it dismissed vs. relied on, and why); G43 mechanically checks the producer SELF-DECLARED symmetry and that dismissed refs each carry a ground.

dismissed.description: Evidence the producer down-weighted or set aside, each with the GROUND for dismissal (e.g. small n, retracted, off-population). A dismissed ref with no ground is a G43 violation.

ground.description: Why it was dismissed (the methodological ground).

relied.description: Evidence the producer relied on to support the claim.

symmetric.description: The producer's self-declaration that the SAME methodological bar was applied to dismissed and relied-upon evidence (e.g. did not dismiss an RCT for small n while relying on a smaller cohort). G43 surfaces this; a false here with no rationale, or dismissed refs lacking grounds, is the violation.

rationale.description: One-paragraph explanation of the skepticism bar applied. Surfaced in the G43 evidence payload.

pooled_estimate.description: Present when the claim pools effect estimates across multiple sources/agents (a quantitative synthesis). G43/G17 check heterogeneity was not hidden: if multiple agents/sources are pooled with high I², `heterogeneity_flagged` must be true. Distinct from the legacy `meta_analysis` object that G17 reads for formal meta-analyses — `pooled_estimate` is the lighter cross-agent pooling case.

agents.description: The experts/agents/sources whose estimates were pooled (e.g. ['Aviv', 'Bert'] or source ids). ≥2 ⇒ pooling occurred.

i2.description: I² heterogeneity statistic. Accepts 0.0-1.0 or 0-100 (consumer normalises). High I² (>50/75) without heterogeneity_flagged is the hidden-heterogeneity failure.

heterogeneity_flagged.description: True if the producer surfaced the heterogeneity to the reader. G43/G17 require this true when i2 is high and ≥2 agents were pooled.

meta_analysis.description: LEGACY (v1) field still consumed by G17 for FORMAL meta-analysis claims: {i_squared|i2, model, warning_marker|note}. Retained for back-compat. New cross-agent pooling should populate `pooled_estimate` instead; both may coexist.
